meta.title: psyche

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: psyche
root.mentor_note: psyche keeps every word it builds pointed inward, toward the mind rather than the body.

attempt.direction_prompt: A physical injury to the body, or a disorder of the mind?

context_sentences: Her psychiatrist adjusted the treatment slowly, one small change at a time, over almost a year.
The hospital’s only psychiatrist was booked three months in advance for most of that winter.
The medication was designed to manage psychosis without leaving the patient permanently sedated.
A brief psychosis brought on by sleep deprivation resolved completely once he finally rested.
The fairground’s psychic claimed to know his fortune before he had even sat down.
Twins often report a strange psychic closeness, sensing when the other is in trouble.

construct_options.text: Relating to the mind, or an unusual, unexplained mental ability
Relating to physical strength and athletic ability
Relating to a severe, diagnosed mental illness

context_sentences: The magician’s act was billed as psychokinesis, though every trick had a far simpler explanation.
No experiment has ever reliably demonstrated psychokinesis under properly controlled conditions.

construct_options.text: The supposed ability to move objects using only the mind
A form of therapy used to treat anxiety and stress
The scientific study of motion and physical forces
